office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: The field includes court administrator Ida Keeley, ferry employee Lacey Sauvageau, former representative Robert J Sutherland, and former councilmember Steve Ewing.

candidates.summary: Keeley is a superior-court administrator emphasizing affordability, public safety, reproductive rights, gun-violence prevention, labor, and environmental protection.
highlights: Supports reproductive rights, gun-violence prevention, and environmental protection [S2].

experience.basis: She has court-administration experience but no documented elected tenure.

candidates.summary: Sauvageau is a ferry employee, former dispatcher and police volunteer, and union negotiator emphasizing ferries, safety, workers, affordability, and accountability.
highlights: Brings emergency-response and labor experience and emphasizes accountable government [S2].

safety.basis: She explicitly emphasizes public safety and has emergency-dispatch experience.

experience.basis: She has operational and union experience but no documented elected tenure.

candidates.summary: Sutherland is a former state representative emphasizing lower taxes and spending, gun and property rights, public safety, parental rights, and limits on mandates.
highlights: Supports lower taxes and spending, parental rights, public safety, and limits on state mandates [S2].

experience.basis: He is a former state representative seeking to return to office.

local-control.basis: His opposition to state mandates supplies a local-control direction.

candidates.summary: Ewing is a former Lake Stevens councilmember and board member emphasizing safety, transportation, housing supply, tax restraint, local government, and small businesses.
highlights: Supports housing supply, tax restraint, public safety, and local government [S2].

experience.basis: He has city-council and multiple board-service records.
